claims.summary: A phase-transition receipt can be acknowledged while felt room remains constrained.
claims.disposition: Preserved as direct felt evidence; receipt state never propagates relief, consent, or closure.

claims.summary: A right-to-ignore invitation may still feel obligatory in a dense context.
claims.disposition: Routed to bounded felt review of delivery texture; silence remains neutral and cannot resolve the concern.

claims.summary: Phase transitions need explicit hold, needs-time, protected-time, and revisit affordances.
claims.disposition: Verified in the current phase-transition, Agency Commons, and protected-time source paths.

claims.summary: Transition identity and reply state should remain durable across the exchange.
claims.disposition: Verified deterministic transition IDs, shared references, reply states, and append-only felt-review receipts.

claims.summary: Transition timing should automatically dilate when density is high.
claims.disposition: Held for Tier 5 operator approval because automatic dilation changes live timing and scheduling behavior.
